rationale: RxJava-specific integration bug: Rx request(0) triggers IllegalArgumentException in async client; not a spec conformance issue.
Dependency upgrade for RxJava and Mongo async driver; no spec behavior involved.
API deprecation of inline aggregate result methods; internal API change, no spec rule violated.
Dependency update to Java Driver Async 3.5; no spec behavior involved.
Dependency update to RxJava 1.3.0; no spec behavior involved.
API deprecation of modifiers in FindObservable; internal API ergonomics change, no spec rule violated.
Auth spec requires mechanism names 'MONGODB-CR' and 'MONGODB-X509'; driver had typos missing 'DB', causing wrong code path.
Driver queries system.indexes instead of using listIndexes command as required by MongoDB 3.0 wire protocol.
Driver inserts into system.indexes instead of using createIndex command as required by MongoDB 3.0 wire protocol.
Dial timeout not honored during initial cluster sync; implementation-level timeout behavior, no specific spec rule named.
Improvement with no description; Depends: DRIVERS-69 indicates spec coordination but insufficient detail to classify further.
Improvement with no description; Depends: DRIVERS-197 suggests proactive spec rollout, not a pre-existing conformance bug.
Socket read concurrency bug causing data corruption under packet loss; implementation flaw, no spec rule contradicted.
Feature addition: JSON serialization for bson.D; not a spec conformance bug.
Documentation improvement for DialInfo Timeout behavior; no spec behavior involved.
Bulk write result field named 'nUpdated' instead of spec-required 'nMatched'; direct spec conformance fix.
GridFS spec requires unique compound index on chunks.files_id+n; driver creates non-unique index and ignores creation failure.
Build failure: internal package import not allowed under Go 1.6; build/packaging issue, no spec behavior.
Server selection spec requires Secondary read preference to work against mongos; driver incorrectly blocks it with slaveOk clause.
Improvement implementing new GridFS spec methods (open_upload_stream_with_id); Depends: DRIVERS-299, proactive spec rollout.
Bug in internal version comparison utility BuildInfo.VersionAtLeast; no spec-defined behavior involved.
Extra bson.Unmarshal in debug logging path; internal performance issue, no spec behavior change.
BSON spec requires Decimal128 clamping and rounding; driver lacked this support; summary confirms spec requirement.
Driver uses legacy OP_GET_MORE for aggregation cursors instead of required getMore command; fails on read-only views in 3.4.
Performance degradation in BSON decoder for find/getMore; internal performance optimization, no spec rule violated.
Implementing bson-corpus specification tests from the spec repo; test infrastructure work.
JSON panic on space between key and colon in bson.UnmarshalJSON; internal JSON parser bug, not BSON spec violation.
Panic prevention in Collection.NewIter when error set and sockets nil; internal defensive coding, no spec rule.
Internal goroutine management improvement for pinger; no spec behavior involved.
SCRAM-SHA-256 spec (DRIVERS-470) specifies not to SASLprep usernames; driver was doing so incorrectly.
Deadlock from double mutex acquisition in Iter.Next on socket error; concurrency implementation bug, no spec rule contradicted.
Deadlock from Wait/Broadcast sync issue in Iter.Next; concurrency implementation bug, no spec rule contradicted.
Interoperability feature: marshaling between mgo and mongo-go-driver BSON types; not a spec conformance issue.
Tailable cursor timeout miscalculation when getMore already running; implementation-level timing bug, no specific spec rule.
NoCursorTimeout option not set in findCmd; driver fails to forward spec-defined cursor option to server.
High DNS request rate from aggressive 100ms retry interval; performance/configuration issue, no spec rule violated.
Panic when index keys are NumberLong instead of float; internal type-handling bug, no spec rule contradicted.
PyMongo dependency version bump; no spec behavior involved.
MotorPool fails to clear prior timeout causing immediate expiry; internal pool implementation bug, no specific spec rule.
Replica set client double-throws AutoReconnect after auth network error; async framework reconnect implementation bug.
pip install version mismatch for pymongo/motor; packaging dependency issue.
Motor deadlock under parallel requests due to monitor lock in pymongo; async framework integration concurrency bug.
Documentation style update; no spec behavior involved.
cursor.close() in asyncio callback doesn't stop iteration due to coroutine semantics; async framework API issue.
Test failures in Tornado 3 due to framework version incompatibility; CI/test framework issue.
Replacing hacky cursor-close tests with MockupDB; internal test infrastructure improvement, not UTF/YAML spec tests.
socketKeepAlive option not working in asyncio Motor; async framework socket option integration bug.
Forward-port of MOTOR-58 with no description; insufficient detail to classify.
Dropping cursor __getitem__ indexing API; intentional breaking API change, no spec conformance issue.
